applicant_profile: Single mother, age 32, two dependents (ages 4 and 7), household income $24,500/year, employed part-time, no current benefits, resides in urban zip code 30312
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Gross monthly income of $2,042 is below 130% FPL threshold of $2,311 for household of 3. Net income after standard deduction and dependent care deduction likely qualifies. Estimated monthly benefit: $516 based on Thrifty Food Plan.
policy_reference: 7 USC 2014 / 7 CFR 273.9 - SNAP Income Eligibility Standards

applicant_profile: Veteran, age 58, served 8 years active duty Army (1990-1998), honorable discharge, service-connected lower back injury rated at 40%, currently unemployed, no private health insurance
expected_eligibility: Eligible for VA disability compensation at 40% rating ($735.71/month as of 2025 rates). Qualifies for Priority Group 3 VA healthcare enrollment. May apply for Individual Unemployability (TDIU) if back condition prevents substantial gainful employment.

applicant_profile: Married couple, both age 68, combined Social Security income $3,100/month, total assets $8,200 (excluding home), prescription drug costs averaging $450/month, residing in Pennsylvania
benefit_type: Medicare Part D Low-Income Subsidy (Extra Help)
expected_eligibility: Eligible for partial Low-Income Subsidy. Combined income of $37,200/year falls below 150% FPL for couple ($30,060 full subsidy threshold exceeded, but below 150% FPL at $45,090). Assets of $8,200 below $37,600 resource limit for couple. Reduced premiums, deductibles, and copayments apply.

applicant_profile: Recent college graduate, age 23, single, no dependents, income $0 (unemployed 3 months), residing with parents who claim her as dependent, parents' AGI $92,000
expected_eligibility: Eligibility depends on state. In Medicaid expansion states, applicant's own household (MAGI-based) may qualify if income is below 138% FPL ($20,783 for individual). Being claimed as a tax dependent complicates MAGI household composition. In non-expansion states, childless adults under 65 are generally ineligible regardless of income.

applicant_profile: Disabled adult, age 41, congenital blindness, unable to perform substantial gainful activity, no work history, no spouse, total assets $1,800, residing in group home in Ohio
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Meets statutory blindness definition (20/200 or less in better eye with correction). No SGA. Countable resources of $1,800 below $2,000 individual limit. Federal benefit rate: $943/month (2025) plus Ohio state supplement. In-kind support from group home may reduce benefit under Value of One-Third Reduction rule.

applicant_profile: Family of 5 (two parents, three children ages 2, 6, 10), household income $52,000/year, employer offers insurance but family coverage costs 12% of household income, residing in Texas
expected_eligibility: Children likely eligible for Texas CHIP. Texas CHIP income limit is 211% FPL for children. For family of 5, 211% FPL is approximately $66,900. Family income of $52,000 qualifies. Employer coverage affordability test may also open Marketplace subsidies for parents under ACA family glitch fix.

applicant_profile: Small business owner, age 45, sole proprietor, reported net self-employment income of $18,000 last year, no employees, lost 60% revenue due to natural disaster in declared county
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Business is located in presidentially declared disaster area. Sole proprietors qualify for EIDL. Maximum loan amount based on actual economic injury up to $2M. Must demonstrate inability to meet obligations and pay ordinary operating expenses. Credit history will be evaluated; minimum credit score 570.
policy_reference: 13 CFR 123.300 / SBA SOP 50 30 - Disaster Loan Program

applicant_profile: Single parent, age 28, one child age 3, household income $16,000/year from part-time retail work, received SNAP and Medicaid, no other benefits, residing in Michigan
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Filing as Head of Household with one qualifying child, AGI of $16,000 falls within EITC phase-in range. Estimated federal EITC: approximately $3,200. Also eligible for Michigan state EITC at 30% of federal credit ($960). Combined refundable credit approximately $4,160.
policy_reference: 26 USC 32 / IRS Publication 596 - Earned Income Credit

applicant_profile: Elderly widow, age 79, Social Security survivor benefit $1,450/month, total assets $3,200, needs assistance with bathing, dressing, and meal preparation, resides alone in apartment in Florida
benefit_type: Medicaid Long-Term Services and Supports (LTSS) - Home and Community-Based Services Waiver
expected_eligibility: Likely eligible for Florida Statewide Medicaid Managed Care Long-Term Care program. Income of $1,450/month falls below Florida's institutional care income limit ($2,829/month). Assets below $2,000 threshold (must spend down $1,200). Requires Level of Care determination showing nursing facility level need. HCBS waiver would cover personal care, homemaker services, and adult day health.

applicant_profile: Recently arrived refugee family from Syria, parents age 35 and 33, two children ages 8 and 11, admitted under USRAP, no employment yet, resides in Minnesota with voluntary agency sponsor
benefit_type: Refugee Cash Assistance (RCA) and Refugee Medical Assistance (RMA)
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Family entered under valid refugee status within past 8 months. Qualifies for RCA at state TANF-equivalent rates for up to 8 months from date of arrival. Children and parents eligible for RMA for 8 months or until Medicaid eligibility is established. Also eligible for ORR-funded employment services and English language training.
policy_reference: 8 USC 1522 / 45 CFR 400 - Office of Refugee Resettlement Programs

applicant_profile: Unemployed construction worker, age 50, laid off after 12 years with same employer due to company downsizing, earned $62,000 last year, filed initial claim in Illinois, no disqualifying separations
expected_eligibility: Eligible. Involuntary separation due to lack of work is qualifying. Illinois base period wages exceed minimum ($1,600 minimum; applicant's base period wages approximately $62,000). Weekly benefit amount estimated at $584 (maximum Illinois WBA). Eligible for up to 26 weeks of regular UI benefits. Must complete work search requirements and biweekly certifications.
